request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose sucrose cryoprotectant HeLa post-thaw viability
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

references.title: Effect of Trehalose and Sucrose in Post-thaw Quality of Crassostrea angulata Sperm
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses the effects of trehalose and sucrose on post-thaw quality, which is relevant to the proposed hypothesis about trehalose as a cryoprotectant.

references.title: Effects of trehalose supplementation on post-thaw sperm quality of honey bee drones
references.relevance_note: This study examines the use of DMSO as a cryoprotectant in honey bee sperm, providing insights into its comparative effectiveness, which may inform the proposed comparison with DMSO in HeLa cells.

references.title: Effects of co-supplementation of trehalose with fetuin and taxifolin in freezing extender on quality parameters of bull spermatozoa following freeze-thaw process.
references.relevance_note: This paper explores the effects of trehalose in combination with other agents on bull sperm quality, which may provide insights into the potential benefits of trehalose in cryopreservation, although it does not focus on HeLa cells.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention of replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant is similar to the studies on trehalose's effects on post-thaw viability in various biological systems, such as sperm cells (papers [2], [4], and [5]). However, these studies do not specifically address HeLa cells or the direct comparison with DMSO protocols. Thus, while there is related work on trehalose, it does not match the exact biological system or the specific experimental context of HeLa cells.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant on the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different cryoprotectant protocols: one with trehalose and one with DMSO. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion assay. The experiment will compare the viability rates between the two groups to determine if trehalose provides superior protection.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.
HeLa cells frozen without any cryoprotectant in freezing medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 100 mL of 10% (w/v) trehalose solution in sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Trehalose solution is clear and free of particulates.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 100 mL of 10% (v/v) DMSO solution in sterile freezing medium (FBS with 10% DMSO) at room temperature.
Expected outcome: DMSO solution is clear and free of particulates.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in a T-75 flask with DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until 80% confluence is reached (approximately 48 h).
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluence.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA solution and neutralize with complete DMEM at 37°C for 5 min.
Expected outcome: Cells are detached and suspended in complete DMEM.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Cell Centrifugation
Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature to pellet the cells.
Expected outcome: Cell pellet is visible at the bottom of the tube.

DAY 1 | 5 min | Cryoprotectant Treatment
Resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of either 10% trehalose solution or 10% DMSO solution, depending on the treatment group.
Expected outcome: Cells are evenly resuspended in the cryoprotectant solution.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Transfer 1 mL of the cell suspension into cryovials and place them in a controlled-rate freezer (Planer Kryo 10) for a programmed cooling rate of -1°C/min until -80°C.
Expected outcome: Cells are frozen to -80°C without ice crystal formation.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Storage
Transfer cryovials to liquid nitrogen storage for long-term preservation.
Expected outcome: Cryovials are stored in liquid nitrogen.

DAY 7 | 2 min | Thawing
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately thaw in a 37°C water bath for 2 min until just thawed.
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed rapidly without overheating.

DAY 7 | 2 h | Post-thaw Recovery
Transfer the thawed cell suspension to a 15 mL tube containing 9 mL of complete DMEM and incubate at 37°C, 5% CO2 for 2 h.
Expected outcome: Cells are allowed to recover post-thaw.

DAY 7 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Perform trypan blue exclusion assay by mixing 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL of 0.4% trypan blue and counting viable cells using a hemocytometer.
Expected outcome: Viability percentage is calculated based on the number of viable cells.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Analyze post-thaw viability data using a two-tailed t-test to compare the means of the trehalose and DMSO groups.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined if the difference in viability is at least 15 percentage points.

assumptions: Trehalose provides better membrane stabilization than sucrose at low temperatures.
HeLa cells will survive the cryopreservation process with both cryoprotectants.
Post-thaw viability can be accurately measured using trypan blue exclusion.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is at least 15 percentage points higher than with DMSO.
Proceed if cell recovery after thawing is >70% for both treatments.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows p < 0.05 between groups.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of trehalose and DMSO for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (between 10-15) for all HeLa cells used.
Calibrate the controlled-rate freezer before each experiment.

phases.phase_name: Day 7: Viability Assessment and Data Analysis

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant on the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.

Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
